briefing.opening: Good morning, everyone. I will begin today's climate briefing for May 31, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Warning of Deepening Domestic Water Shortage Crisis: Water Shortage Projected to Increase Eightfold by 2030
sections.content: Today, both domestic and international news commonly warned of Korea's severe water shortage crisis. Even without considering the impacts of climate change, water shortages are expected to occur by 2030, and if climate change is factored in, the water shortage is projected to surge eightfold compared to current levels [1, 3, 10, 45]. Furthermore, the financial resources required to resolve the water shortage are also analyzed to increase sixfold, necessitating urgent measures [2, 5].

sections.title: 🌱 Spreading Carbon Neutrality Practices in Daily Life and Exploring New Alternatives
sections.content: In positive news, various efforts to practice carbon neutrality are spreading domestically. BGF Retail has embarked on forest creation to prevent desertification in Mongolia [7, 8], and Hyundai Department Store, in cooperation with the Ministry of Climate, Energy, and Environment, is conducting an eco-friendly campaign that offers double mileage and points for practicing carbon neutrality [11, 16, 17, 19, 23, 31, 32]. Additionally, Gwangmyeong City's Climate Volunteers and Mungyeong Neulbom Family Volunteer Group are leading carbon neutrality practices in daily life through on-site education and experiential activities [15, 20, 21, 22, 24], and the Gyeongnam Provincial Office of Education is operating an Environmental Education Week to encourage participation [26, 29]. In the energy sector, hydrothermal energy is emerging as a new carbon-neutral alternative that can be supplied 24 hours a day, year-round [14, 25], and Korea Midland Power and Sejong Facilities Management Corporation are actively participating in achieving carbon neutrality by promoting solar power projects utilizing idle land [27].

sections.title: 🌱 Efforts to Expand Renewable Energy and Secure Policy Flexibility
sections.content: Concrete movements to expand renewable energy are also being observed. Jeju Province announced that through its 7th Regional Energy Plan, it will focus on expanding renewable energy and securing flexibility in the energy system [33]. Rooftop solar power is particularly gaining attention as a key battleground for achieving the 2030 solar power generation target, and artificial intelligence (AI) technology is expected to bring about changes in this field [34]. Siheung City's 'RE100' model, an innovative structure that reinvests renewable energy profits into industrial complexes, has garnered significant interest from the diplomatic corps in Korea [35, 37, 38]. Yeongam County is also discussing financial support measures for the creation of a renewable energy complex, demonstrating ongoing nationwide efforts for renewable energy transition [36]. Companies are also actively participating, with Hyosung Heavy Industries reducing carbon emissions at its Changwon plant through solar PPA (Power Purchase Agreement) [39].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Strengthening Climate Change Response Capabilities and Policy Challenges
sections.content: Alongside efforts to strengthen climate change response capabilities, policy challenges are also emerging. A joint training exercise involving 12 organizations was successfully conducted on the Pyeongtaek-Siheung Line to respond to complex highway disasters in the face of climate change [4]. Gyeonggi Province is promoting climate change adaptation policies by urging residents to utilize 'Gyeonggi Climate Insurance,' which covers heatwave damages [6, 9]. Meanwhile, as the transition to carbon neutrality accelerates, there has been a call for urgent and effective support measures [12], and policy discussions are active, with a town hall meeting on carbon-neutral cities held by the 2050 Carbon Neutrality and Green Growth Committee [18]. Ahead of the local elections, candidates such as Kim Jin-kyun and Seong Gwang-jin announced carbon-neutral education policies as pledges, emphasizing the importance of education for future generations [28, 30].

sections.title: 🌍 International Community's Climate Crisis Warnings and Response Movements
sections.content: Warnings and response movements regarding the climate crisis continue within the international community. In Europe, research has shown that climate change is 'supercharging' larger and more destructive hailstones [42], and a warning has also been raised that most of South America's cloud forests could disappear due to climate change [44]. Research also highlights that dead trees are accumulating faster than they decompose in forests, and climate change is accelerating this cycle [43]. Meanwhile, news from Germany reported that gas subsidies and policies suppressing renewable energy are increasing electricity prices, demonstrating the complexity of energy transition policies [40]. Amidst these circumstances, Bizkaia is conducting research on adapting beach seasons to climate change [47], and KPMG is highlighting the net-zero readiness of island regions [48], indicating active international adaptation efforts and research activities [41, 46, 49].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing presented a clear warning about the future crises climate change will bring, while also demonstrating that various domestic and international efforts are simultaneously underway to overcome them. This is a time that requires continuous attention and action. This concludes the climate briefing for May 31, 2026.
